tool_design.role_in_intervention: Point-of-use labelling pack that makes cold-hold and shelf-life decisions visible in service.
tool_design.usage_moment: Whenever items are opened, decanted, or placed out for cold hold
tool_design.expected_output: Visible labels showing opened date, use-by, out-time, or discard time

failure_warnings.warning: The template is available but not completed at the trigger moment.
failure_warnings.what_can_go_wrong: Important evidence is lost because the tool is used too late.
mitigation: Simplify if speed is the issue

failure_warnings.what_can_go_wrong: The output exists but cannot guide action.
early_signals: No owner or due date captured

content:
# Cold-Hold & Date Label Pack **Tool ID:** T002-05 **Block:** B002 (Cold Chain Management SOP) **Domain:** Food Safety & Hygiene (D01_food_safety) **Level:** L1 **Format:** Template **Role:** All Staff **Time Required:** Under 1 minute per label **Version:** 2.0 --- ## What This Tool Is For Use these printable labels to make cold-hold timing and opened-item dating visible at the exact point of use. This tool covers two common blind spots: - food taken out for display without a discard time - opened or decanted items put back into the fridge with no traceable life left on them --- ## Label Rules | Label Type | Use It When | Minimum Fields | |---|---|---| | Cold-hold display label | A chilled item is placed out for service | Product, out time, discard time, initials | | Opened pack label | A sealed product is opened | Product, opened date, use-by, initials | | Decanted / house-made label | Food is transferred or made in-house | Product, made date, use-by, initials | --- ## Quick Check Before Labelling
  • I know whether this item is being displayed, opened, or decanted.
  • I am writing the real discard or use-by time, not "later".
  • The label will stay visible on the item or tray.
--- ## Printable Label Templates ### 1. Cold-Hold Display Label | Field | Write Here | |---|---| | Product | ____________________ | | Put out at | ____________________ | | Discard at | ____________________ | | Initials | ____________________ | ### 2. Opened Pack Label | Field | Write Here | |---|---| | Product | ____________________ | | Opened on | ____________________ | | Use by | ____________________ | | Initials | ____________________ | ### 3. Decanted / House-Made Item Label | Field | Write Here | |---|---| | Product | ____________________ | | Made / decanted on | ____________________ | | Use by | ____________________ | | Initials | ____________________ | --- ## House Rules To Print Beside The Labels - Time-label chilled display items the moment they leave refrigeration. - Do not re-chill and restart the clock after the display limit is reached. - Do not store unlabeled opened dairy, fillings, sauces, or desserts. - If you cannot confirm when something was opened or put out, do not guess. Escalate and discard if needed. --- ## Best Uses In A Cafe - Sandwiches and rolls placed into a grab-and-go display - Milk or cream opened for service prep - Sauces, spreads, or fillings transferred into squeeze bottles or gastro tubs - Cake slices or chilled desserts moved into service display trays --- ## Connected Resources **Used with:** - T002-01 - Delivery Acceptance Checklist - T002-02 - Cold Chain SOP & Fridge Layout Guide - T002-04 - Weekly Fridge Organisation & Date Labelling Audit **Related blocks:** - B001 - Daily Temperature Monitoring --- *Tool Version 2.0 - a tiny tool that prevents a lot of "how long has this been here?" moments.*
